title: Specklinia tenera
taxonomy: Specklinia tenera is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it among the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Specklinia tenera is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes many epiphytic orchids. The genus Specklinia is a part of the tribe Epidendreae, characterized by its unique flower structure. Within this tribe, Specklinia tenera is classified as a species within the genus Specklinia.

intro: Specklinia tenera, also known as the delicate orchid or tender specklinia, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Specklinia tenera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Specklinia tenera is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
